mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing the present value of equity claims and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0 percent, it signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations downward over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean 20-day return when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production indicates manufacturing capacity utilization is expanding, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following an INDPRO increase are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stationary federal funds rate compresses real interest rates and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3 percent month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the real rate compression signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple inflation cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive payroll revisions are negative or exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling period, invalidating the earnings linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms sustained aggregate demand growth, which reduces recession probabilities and drives positive equity market performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than half of triggered observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a synchronized contraction in output and labor demand, which triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls month-over-month while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 50 percent of synchronized decline instances, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during confirmed contraction periods.
